behavior_summary: Handles the 'U8P_DAGGER::look' (examine) process for the DAGGER item. When the player examines the dagger, the script emits a single short description bark "dagger " and then suspends/ends the U8P_DAGGER::look process.
There is no branching, menu, or external call behavior; the routine's sole observable effect is the one-line description output.

Item-level metadata (not part of the runtime script nodes) appears to include presentation hints such as overlay.animationStyle = 'dagger' and usedBy = ['dagger','korghin'], which likely affect rendering/usage elsewhere but are not referenced by this script.
notable_control_flow: U8P_DAGGER::look executes as a process (processType 0x0208), performs one Bark node producing the text 'dagger ', then reaches a suspend/terminate point — effectively a straight-line emit-and-stop flow.
No conditional branches, loops, or calls are present in the decompiled node sequence; control flow is immediately linear from entry to suspend.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text 'dagger ' intentional (formatting artifact) or should it be 'dagger'?
Are the item metadata fields (overlay.animationStyle and usedBy) consumed by other systems at runtime, and should they be considered part of this object's behavior profile? (inference only)
Was any richer description intended but stripped down to a single bark in this asset's data?

main_function.name: U8P_DAGGER::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description shown when a player examines the DAGGER item; emit a single bark and end the U8P_DAGGER::look process.
entry_conditions: Player invokes the 'U8P_DAGGER::look' (examine) verb on the DAGGER item.
Engine dispatches a U8P_DAGGER::look/processType 0x0208 event for this item.

function_map.name: U8P_DAGGER::look
function_map.role: Handle the item's U8P_DAGGER::look/examine event by emitting a single short description bark and suspending.
